title: Network Information API for adaptive image/video quality based on connection type
viewport_spectrum: All — concern is connection quality not viewport size
scenario_context: Video feed adapts quality based on connection: 4G HD, 3G SD, 2G/saveData skips video entirely. Network Information API + saveData flag drive decisions.
responsive_reasoning: 1. Connection quality is viewport context most apps ignore.\n2. Adaptive bitrate streaming is gold standard; simpler apps can use conditional source.\n3. navigator.connection on Chrome/Edge; absent on Safari (fallback to default quality).

layout_strategy.information_density_changes: Lower quality on slow connections

performance_notes: responsive-121 keeps Network Information API for adaptive image/video quality based on connection type responsive by bounding layout work, memoizing derived measurements, avoiding viewport-width font scaling, and adding a viewport or container regression check for viewport detection and reaction.
accessibility_across_viewports: Across viewports, Network Information API for adaptive image/video quality based on connection type keeps focus order, labels, hit targets, content order, and error/status messaging equivalent even when layout density changes.
edge_cases_handled: User toggles saveData mid-session — connection.change fires
Safari (no API) — defaults to high quality
what_breaks_naive_approach: A naive layout for Network Information API for adaptive image/video quality based on connection type treats All — concern is connection quality not viewport size as one width breakpoint. It breaks the viewport detection and reaction invariant when safe areas, zoom, text length, keyboard height, pointer type, orientation, or container size change independently.
what_makes_this_truly_responsive: 1) Network quality is first-class context. 2) Reactive to connection changes. 3) User preference respected.

title: Navigation tabs that overflow into a more menu when they don't fit container
viewport_spectrum: Tabs in narrow vs wide containers
scenario_context: Tab bar with 6+ tabs. In wide container, all visible. In narrow, overflow into 'More' dropdown. Detection via JS measurement (can't be CSS-only for visibility-based overflow).
responsive_reasoning: 1. Tab overflow is a layout concern that needs JS measurement.\n2. ResizeObserver on tab bar measures available width.\n3. Show tabs until overflow, rest in dropdown.

detection_strategy.ssr_considerations: Render all SSR; client measures and overflows

layout_strategy.information_density_changes: Narrow shows fewer tabs + More

performance_notes: responsive-122 keeps Navigation tabs that overflow into a more menu when they don't fit container responsive by bounding layout work, memoizing derived measurements, avoiding viewport-width font scaling, and adding a viewport or container regression check for container queries mastery.
accessibility_across_viewports: role=tab/tablist (tab in production for tablist). More menu has role=menu/menuitem.
edge_cases_handled: Many tabs in narrow — all but 1-2 go to More
All tabs fit — More button not shown
Active tab in overflow — More button indicates (could add badge)
what_breaks_naive_approach: A naive layout for Navigation tabs that overflow into a more menu when they don't fit container treats Tabs in narrow vs wide containers as one width breakpoint. It breaks the container queries mastery invariant when safe areas, zoom, text length, keyboard height, pointer type, orientation, or container size change independently.
what_makes_this_truly_responsive: 1) JS measures actual fit. 2) Overflow accessible via menu. 3) Adapts to container resize.

title: Article reading mode with adjustable font size, persisted per user
scenario_context: Article reader with A- / A+ buttons to adjust font size. User's preference persists across sessions. Base font scales accordingly.
anti_patterns_to_avoid: Resetting font on every page load
Fixed scale that doesn't honor user agent base size
responsive_reasoning: 1. User control over font size is core accessibility.\n2. Set --base-font on html element; entire scale follows via rem.\n3. localStorage persists.

detection_strategy.ssr_considerations: Inline script applies preference before render

layout_strategy.information_density_changes: Larger font = less per screen

interaction_model_per_device.desktop_keyboard: Cmd++ / Cmd+- could be intercepted (browser zoom is also OK)

performance_notes: responsive-123 keeps Article reading mode with adjustable font size, persisted per user responsive by bounding layout work, memoizing derived measurements, avoiding viewport-width font scaling, and adding a viewport or container regression check for fluid typography systems.
accessibility_across_viewports: role=group with aria-label. Buttons have aria-label. Live region announces current size. Touch targets 44pt.
what_breaks_naive_approach: A naive layout for Article reading mode with adjustable font size, persisted per user treats Reading on any device as one width breakpoint. It breaks the fluid typography systems invariant when safe areas, zoom, text length, keyboard height, pointer type, orientation, or container size change independently.
what_makes_this_truly_responsive: 1) User-controlled scale. 2) Persists across sessions. 3) CSS variable architecture allows easy theming.

title: Margin-collapse-aware spacing using grid gap instead of margin
scenario_context: Vertical stack of varied components. Margins between them collapse and create inconsistent spacing. Using grid/flex gap eliminates this.
responsive_reasoning: 1. Margin collapse is a CSS gotcha.\n2. gap on flex/grid containers doesn't collapse.\n3. Predictable spacing.

detection_strategy.code_pattern: display: flex; flex-direction: column; gap: var(--space-md);

layout_strategy.information_density_changes: Predictable

interaction_model_per_device.mobile_touch: Mobile touch uses a single-column flow, 44px targets, safe-area padding, and no hover-only actions for Margin-collapse-aware spacing using grid gap instead of margin.
interaction_model_per_device.tablet: Tablet uses a two-pane layout when width allows, keeps 44px targets, and preserves source order for Margin-collapse-aware spacing using grid gap instead of margin.
interaction_model_per_device.desktop_mouse: Desktop mouse gets hover as enhancement only; all controls remain visible/clickable for Margin-collapse-aware spacing using grid gap instead of margin.
interaction_model_per_device.desktop_keyboard: Keyboard follows DOM order, visible focus rings, and Escape/Enter behavior for Margin-collapse-aware spacing using grid gap instead of margin.

performance_notes: responsive-124 keeps Margin-collapse-aware spacing using grid gap instead of margin responsive by bounding layout work, memoizing derived measurements, avoiding viewport-width font scaling, and adding a viewport or container regression check for fluid spacing and layout systems.

what_breaks_naive_approach: A naive layout for Margin-collapse-aware spacing using grid gap instead of margin treats Any layout requiring consistent spacing as one width breakpoint. It breaks the fluid spacing and layout systems invariant when safe areas, zoom, text length, keyboard height, pointer type, orientation, or container size change independently.
what_makes_this_truly_responsive: 1) Predictable gap. 2) Fluid via clamp. 3) Composable.

title: Stepper input (number with +/- buttons) sized for touch, with keyboard input for typing
scenario_context: Quantity input. Number input has +/- buttons for touch. Type-in for desktop. Touch targets 44pt; desktop can be tighter.
anti_patterns_to_avoid: Only buttons (slow for desktop power users)
responsive_reasoning: 1. Both input methods available — buttons for touch, typing for keyboard/mouse.\n2. Button size adapts to pointer.\n3. Native input handles typing.

layout_strategy.primary_technique: Combined input + buttons with adaptive sizing
layout_strategy.information_density_changes: Touch larger; mouse compact

interaction_model_per_device.mobile_touch: Tap +/- buttons (44pt), or tap input to type
interaction_model_per_device.desktop_keyboard: Arrow up/down to inc/dec, or type

edge_cases_handled: Keyboard user — arrows up/down work natively
Type 'abc' — coerced to min via Number conversion
what_breaks_naive_approach: A naive layout for Stepper input (number with +/- buttons) sized for touch, with keyboard input for typing treats Quantity selectors on shopping sites as one width breakpoint. It breaks the touch vs mouse input models invariant when safe areas, zoom, text length, keyboard height, pointer type, orientation, or container size change independently.
what_makes_this_truly_responsive: 1) Pointer-aware button size. 2) Native input for typing. 3) Multiple input methods.

title: Modal that closes if user opens app's native keyboard but stays if they keep typing
scenario_context: Quick reply modal with a text input. User types, keyboard appears. User might dismiss keyboard via swipe-down — modal should stay (user might want to format text). User taps backdrop = explicit close intent.
responsive_reasoning: 1. Keyboard dismiss != close intent.\n2. Backdrop tap, X, Escape are close intents.\n3. Per responsive-082 already.

detection_strategy.code_pattern: // Do not close on visualViewport change

interaction_model_per_device.desktop_mouse: Click backdrop or X to close

accessibility_across_viewports: role=dialog aria-modal aria-label. Escape closes. Explicit close button.

User submits — onSubmit fires, parent closes
User scrolls inside modal — no conflict
what_breaks_naive_approach: A naive layout for Modal that closes if user opens app's native keyboard but stays if they keep typing treats Mobile with modal forms as one width breakpoint. It breaks the soft keyboard handling invariant when safe areas, zoom, text length, keyboard height, pointer type, orientation, or container size change independently.

title: Safe area aware floating tooltip that doesn't appear behind notch in landscape
scenario_context: Tooltip positions itself near trigger. In landscape with notch on left, tooltip near left edge can appear behind notch. Position calculation must consider safe areas.
anti_patterns_to_avoid: Hardcoded position without safe area awareness
responsive_reasoning: 1. Safe areas constrain available positioning space.\n2. Use env() values in JS or CSS positioning math.\n3. Floating-ui or manual collision detection.

layout_strategy.information_density_changes: Narrow containers show summaries first; wider containers reveal progressive detail for Safe area aware floating tooltip that doesn't appear behind notch in landscape.

title: Drawing canvas that resizes on rotation without losing content
scenario_context: Drawing app with canvas. When user rotates, canvas resizes to new dimensions but content must be preserved.
responsive_reasoning: 1. Canvas resize is destructive — must save and restore.\n2. Save imageData before resize, redraw after.\n3. Handle DPR per responsive-047.

title: Multi-window iPad (Stage Manager) — each window has independent state
viewport_spectrum: iPad with two app windows side by side via Stage Manager
scenario_context: Stage Manager lets users open the same web app in two windows side by side. Each window has its own state, URL, scroll position.
anti_patterns_to_avoid: Singleton state shared across windows (BroadcastChannel could intentionally do this, but usually no)
localStorage causing state pollution across windows
responsive_reasoning: 1. Each window is independent — different URL, state, viewport.\n2. matchMedia per window reports actual width.\n3. localStorage shared (caution); sessionStorage per window.

detection_strategy.code_pattern: Standard responsive code works — Stage Manager just changes viewport size

layout_strategy.primary_technique: Standard responsive design — Stage Manager doesn't need special handling
layout_strategy.information_density_changes: Window width drives layout

interaction_model_per_device.tablet: Stage Manager respects all responsive code
interaction_model_per_device.desktop_mouse: Desktop mouse gets hover as enhancement only; all controls remain visible/clickable for Multi-window iPad (Stage Manager) — each window has independent state.
interaction_model_per_device.desktop_keyboard: Keyboard follows DOM order, visible focus rings, and Escape/Enter behavior for Multi-window iPad (Stage Manager) — each window has independent state.

accessibility_across_viewports: Across viewports, Multi-window iPad (Stage Manager) — each window has independent state keeps focus order, labels, hit targets, content order, and error/status messaging equivalent even when layout density changes.
edge_cases_handled: Two windows of same app — independent state via sessionStorage
User resizes one window — only that window's layout adapts
User opens link in same window — sessionStorage preserved
User opens link in new window — fresh sessionStorage
what_breaks_naive_approach: A naive layout for Multi-window iPad (Stage Manager) — each window has independent state treats iPad with two app windows side by side via Stage Manager as one width breakpoint. It breaks the the tablet problem invariant when safe areas, zoom, text length, keyboard height, pointer type, orientation, or container size change independently.
what_makes_this_truly_responsive: 1) Width-driven responsive — Stage Manager 'just works'. 2) sessionStorage for per-window state. 3) Standard matchMedia patterns.

title: Outline view that's hidden by default mobile, peek-revealed on tablet, persistent on desktop
scenario_context: Article with section outline (table of contents). Mobile: button reveals as drawer. Tablet: peek (narrow column visible on right). Desktop: persistent right column.

It breaks the multi pane layouts invariant when safe areas, zoom, text length, keyboard height, pointer type, orientation, or container size change independently.

title: Tree table that shows expand/collapse with adaptive indentation per viewport

title: Multi-line textarea with autosize that handles paste of long content gracefully

title: Backstack-aware navigation: back button works like browser AND app history

anti_patterns_to_avoid: Custom back buttons that don't sync with browser
Modal opens with no back-button handling (back closes wrong thing)
responsive_reasoning: 1. Browser back is user's universal back gesture.\n2. SPAs must integrate with history API.\n3. Modal opens push state so back closes modal.

title: Display text with text-wrap: balance for headlines that look intentional
scenario_context: Marketing headline 'Build faster with our incredible new platform'. At certain widths it wraps as 'Build faster with our incredible new \n platform' — one word on the second line looks bad. text-wrap: balance distributes lines evenly.
responsive_reasoning: 1. CSS text-wrap: balance computes optimal line distribution.\n2. Use on headlines/titles, not body.\n3. Newer browser support but progressive enhancement.

title: Active state styling that doesn't stick on touch like hover does
scenario_context: Button has active state (pressed look). On touch, after tapping, active state can stick if not handled. Need to ensure clean removal.
anti_patterns_to_avoid: Active state styled like hover (sticks on touch)
No touch feedback at all (feels unresponsive)
responsive_reasoning: 1. :active fires on press; properly removes on release on touch.\n2. Combined with :hover for mouse.\n3. Provide tactile feedback on touch via :active styling.

layout_strategy.information_density_changes: Narrow containers show summaries first; wider containers reveal progressive detail for Active state styling that doesn't stick on touch like hover does.

performance_notes: responsive-141 keeps Active state styling that doesn't stick on touch like hover does responsive by bounding layout work, memoizing derived measurements, avoiding viewport-width font scaling, and adding a viewport or container regression check for touch vs mouse input models.
accessibility_across_viewports: Focus-visible for keyboard. Active for press feedback. Hover for mouse.
edge_cases_handled: iOS default tap flash — removed via WebKit property
what_breaks_naive_approach: A naive layout for Active state styling that doesn't stick on touch like hover does treats Touch devices as one width breakpoint. It breaks the touch vs mouse input models invariant when safe areas, zoom, text length, keyboard height, pointer type, orientation, or container size change independently.
what_makes_this_truly_responsive: 1) Three pseudo-classes covering all input. 2) Tactile feedback. 3) Cleaned default.

title: Login form that maintains layout integrity when password manager fills it
scenario_context: iOS/Android password managers slide up suggestions above keyboard. Custom form layouts can break if they assume keyboard takes specific height. Suggestions strip is extra.
anti_patterns_to_avoid: Layouts that don't account for suggestion strip
responsive_reasoning: 1. Password manager UI adds height above keyboard.\n2. visualViewport already accounts for this.\n3. Don't disable autofill — it's a critical convenience.

detection_strategy.code_pattern: Same visualViewport pattern; bar height included

layout_strategy.content_priority: Inputs reachable above all keyboard chrome
layout_strategy.information_density_changes: Narrow containers show summaries first; wider containers reveal progressive detail for Login form that maintains layout integrity when password manager fills it.

interaction_model_per_device.mobile_touch: Tap input → keyboard + suggestions appear → form scrolls

performance_notes: responsive-142 keeps Login form that maintains layout integrity when password manager fills it responsive by bounding layout work, memoizing derived measurements, avoiding viewport-width font scaling, and adding a viewport or container regression check for soft keyboard handling.
accessibility_across_viewports: Across viewports, Login form that maintains layout integrity when password manager fills it keeps focus order, labels, hit targets, content order, and error/status messaging equivalent even when layout density changes.
edge_cases_handled: iOS password manager suggestion bar — visualViewport.height excludes it, layout adapts
Desktop browser autofill — popup native, no layout impact
Apple Passkey — uses same autoComplete tokens
what_breaks_naive_approach: A naive layout for Login form that maintains layout integrity when password manager fills it treats Mobile with password manager autofill as one width breakpoint. It breaks the soft keyboard handling invariant when safe areas, zoom, text length, keyboard height, pointer type, orientation, or container size change independently.
what_makes_this_truly_responsive: 1) autoComplete tokens enable all autofill. 2) visualViewport accounts for all keyboard chrome. 3) Required fields use native validation.

responsive_reasoning: 1. Camera view full-bleed.\n2. Overlay UI inset via env() per side.\n3. Orientation may change mid-session.

title: Calculator UI that switches scientific vs basic based on orientation
scenario_context: iOS Calculator shows basic in portrait, scientific in landscape. Web calculator can do the same.
anti_patterns_to_avoid: Same calculator in both orientations (wastes landscape)
responsive_reasoning: 1. Landscape gives horizontal space — scientific buttons fit.\n2. Portrait shows essentials.\n3. Same state, more buttons available in landscape.

layout_strategy.primary_technique: Conditional pane via orientation media query
layout_strategy.content_priority: Display + basic always; scientific extra in landscape
layout_strategy.information_density_changes: Landscape adds buttons

performance_notes: responsive-144 keeps Calculator UI that switches scientific vs basic based on orientation responsive by bounding layout work, memoizing derived measurements, avoiding viewport-width font scaling, and adding a viewport or container regression check for orientation and aspect ratio.

It breaks the orientation and aspect ratio invariant when safe areas, zoom, text length, keyboard height, pointer type, orientation, or container size change independently.
what_makes_this_truly_responsive: 1) Orientation reveals functionality. 2) Shared state. 3) Native CSS adaptation.

title: Spreadsheet with frozen rows and columns plus virtual scrolling for huge sheets

responsive_reasoning: 1. Sticky positioning + virtualization combined.\n2. CSS sticky for frozen header/column; JS virtualization for body.\n3. Performance critical at scale.

title: Inline editing in table cells with mobile vs desktop UX
scenario_context: Edit cell value inline. Mobile: tap opens fullscreen sheet with keyboard for clarity. Desktop: input appears in cell.
anti_patterns_to_avoid: Tiny input on mobile cells (hard to type)
Complex modal for simple edit on desktop
responsive_reasoning: 1. Mobile cells are small; sheet provides space.\n2. Desktop has room for inline edit.\n3. Same data; different UX.

title: Color picker with native input on mobile, custom palette on desktop
scenario_context: Form needs color selection. Mobile: native <input type='color'> opens OS picker. Desktop: custom palette with HSL controls for precision.
anti_patterns_to_avoid: Custom picker on mobile (worse than native)
Native input only on desktop (limited precision)
responsive_reasoning: 1. Native picker is platform-tuned on mobile.\n2. Desktop benefits from custom HSL or palette for precision.\n3. Same value stored as hex.

title: Skip-to-content link that becomes visible on focus for keyboard users
scenario_context: Skip-to-content link for keyboard users to bypass header navigation. Visually hidden by default; visible on focus.
anti_patterns_to_avoid: No skip link (keyboard users tab through all nav every page)
responsive_reasoning: 1. Keyboard accessibility requirement.\n2. Visible only on focus.\n3. Same on every viewport (universal need).

layout_strategy.primary_technique: Sr-only by default, visible on focus
